title: Docusaurus Strategy
description: Explores when to pick Docusaurus instead of MkDocs for this project.

questions.question: Which capability is native to Docusaurus but requires extra plugins in MkDocs?
options: MDX components with live React widgets
questions.explanation: Docusaurus ships with MDX/React so interactive components are natural, whereas MkDocs would need custom plugins or embeds.

questions.question: When is it most appropriate to stay on MkDocs instead of migrating?
options: When you only need Markdown SOPs with fast builds
When you need multi-lingual docs and blog features
When you require versioned doc sets for every release
When you must embed React live code sandboxes
questions.explanation: MkDocs excels at straightforward Markdown documentation with minimal tooling overhead.

questions.question: What additional toolchain does Docusaurus introduce?
questions.explanation: Docusaurus builds with Node.js (typically Yarn or npm), unlike MkDocs' Python-only toolchain.
